summary: Matrix blend delivers crystal‑clear sound while the June ’73 set dazzles with a soaring Dark Star and stellar Playing in the Band.
review: The matrix mix of soundboard and audience creates a beautifully balanced recording – crisp drums, deep bass and a warm keyboard texture that lets the band breathe. Musically the show shines with a sweet Dark Star, an outstanding Playing in the Band, and powerful renditions of Sugar Magnolia and Stella Blue, making it one of the stronger 1973 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about both the performance and the matrix recording indicate an exceptional show with excellent audio quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Balanced soundboard/audience blend; nice overall mix, deep bass, crisp Bill Kreutzmann rhythm, not overly bright but very listenable. Reviewers note “beautiful”, “magnificent” and “very good quality”.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as well‑played with an outstanding Playing in the Band and strong country‑rockabilly flavor; second set shorter but still delivers highlights like Dark Star, Eyes of the World and a powerful encore. Overall flow praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals highlighted as great on Black Peter and other numbers; wah‑wah peaks noted during Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as deep and very good for a ’73 recording.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocal fireworks and stellar contributions praised, especially in the first set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's keyboard work stands out – Moog (though debated) and Rhodes tones are singled out as wonderful texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s rhythm called crisp and tight.
